response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role requires deep hardware expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design. Aside from brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services, there is no evidence of computer architecture, GPU/SIMD experience, deep-learning workload optimization, or silicon-level performance work. This is a fundamental domain mismatch for a principal-level hardware performance architect position.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (DLang services handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Long, diverse engineering career (18+ years) spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Demonstrated technical leadership through open-source projects, workshops, and teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, computer architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/application development, not silicon or infrastructure performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Low-latency systems and performance tuning background
